rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK (SOUTH DAKOTA) N.A. VS. SEPIDEH VALENTINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment And Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 11, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT SEPIDEH VALENTINO Motion To Vacate Judgment And Set Aside Default. Denied. Judgment was entered in August 2014. Defendant did not move to vacate the judgment until January 2015. Defendant offers no explanation for the delay. Thus, Defendant has not moved to vacate the judgment within a reasonable time, as required by CCP sec. 473(b). (See, e.g., Benjamino v. Dalmo Mfg. Co. (1948) 31 Cal.2d 523, 528, holding that while the trial court has discretion in determining if the motion is made within a reasonable time period, "there must be some showing-some evidence-as the basis for the exercise of such discretion.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CLEUSENIR DIAS VS. DAVID KAHN MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Cleusenir Dias' Motion To Compel Deposition Of Christina Santana And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday March 11, 2015, Line 3, Plaintiff Cleusenir Dias' Motion To Compel Deposition Of Christina Santana And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion is granted as follows. Defendants shall produce Ms. Santana for deposition within ten days unless Plaintiff's counsel agrees to a later date certain. Sanctions are awarded against defendants in the amount of $2,000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALSCO, A CORPORATION VS. GROUPO RISTORANTE ITALIANO, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Including Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday March 11, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ALSCO, A CORPORATION'S Motion For Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Including Attorneys Fees. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Alsco's Motion For Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Facts Be Admitted is denied in part and granted in part as follows: assuming that the responses served were in "substantial compliance" with CCP 2030.220, then Alsco's Motion is denied. However, monetary sanctions are mandatory under these circumstances, and are ordered in the amount of $750 against defendant Groupo Restorante Italiano, Inc. Alsco's motion to compel Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents is denied, without prejudice. Defendant has apparently served responses, which makes the motion moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CALEB DUBOIS ET AL VS. COMCAST CORPORATION, A PENNSYLVANIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 11, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT COMCAST CABLE COMMUNICATIONS MANAGEMENT, LLC Defendants Motion To Strike Portions Of The Complaint. The motion is granted with 20 days leave to amend as to the tolling allegations. Plaintiff has leave to allege that tolling exists through the date of the decertification order. See Hall v. Variable Annuity Life Ins. Co., 727 F.3d 372, 375 (5th Cir. 2013); Giovanniello v. ALM Media, LLC, 726 F.3d 106, 117 (2nd Cir. 2013. The motion is granted with 20 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action to cure the allegations that are contrary to Brinker Restaurant Corp. v. Sup. Crt. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 1004, 1017. The remainder of the motion is denied. The class decertification ruling was procedural in nature and does not have a collateral estoppel effect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: INSITESOURCE CORPORATION VS. GREGORY ROSSMANN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 11, 2015, Line 14, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. Denied. Cross-Complainants have not established a likelihood of prevailing or that the balance of harms weighs in their favor. As to the likelihood of prevailing, Cross-Complainants have not introduced sufficient evidence to establish that Peter Nordberg was acting as an agent of Montage Capital, LLC ("Montage") at the time the alleged misrepresentations were made. As such, Cross-Complainants have not established that they are likely to prevail against Montage. Further, it is undisputed that Cross-Complainants executed Subordination Agreements, which unambiguously provide that Montage is the senior creditor and that Cross-Complainants may not interfere with the collection of its debt. (Declaration of Michael Rose, Ex. A., pars. 1-3, 7.) As to the balance of harms, Cross-Complainants have not established that money damages will not fully compensate them for their loss. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
